global_entity_library.Bentley: A classic Bentley is shown driving on a scenic road, with a close-up of its luxurious interior featuring a brown leather steering wheel with the iconic winged 'B' emblem, symbolizing luxury and craftsmanship.
global_entity_library.Digital Dashboard Timer: A red digital timer mounted on the dashboard, counting up during the acceleration run, visible in interior shots, which the narration confirms reached 33 seconds when the car hit 400 km/h, and the total run time was 41.96 seconds.
global_entity_library.Bugatti Chiron: A black Bugatti Chiron, partially wrapped in protective film, positioned on a rotating assembly platform in the Bugatti Factory, featuring its signature front grille, quad headlights, and carbon fiber body, with blue cables and protective tape near the wheel arches. A narrator explains that this super car is fully covered and will demonstrate its capabilities, undergoing a 350 km test drive before delivery, all set to dramatic orchestral music.
global_entity_library.Factory Assembly Line: The video shows a modern factory environment with partially assembled white cars and workers in the background, highlighting the blend of handmade precision and machine efficiency in supercar production, with a focus on the advanced manufacturing process.
global_entity_library.Lamborghini Huracán Evo: A blue Lamborghini Huracán Evo is visible in the background of the workshop, partially covered, indicating the presence of other high-performance vehicles in the facility.
global_entity_library.Orange Race Car: An orange race car with the number '63' and 'BLANCPAIN' branding is shown on a racetrack, indicating its use in competitive motorsport and highlighting its aggressive, aerodynamic design.
global_entity_library.Rimac Facility: An aerial view of the Rimac production site is displayed, showcasing a modern, white industrial building with the 'RIMAC' logo, surrounded by greenery and parked vehicles, indicating a high-tech, exclusive production environment.
global_entity_library.Solar-Paneled Industrial Complex: An aerial view of a large industrial facility with extensive solar panels on the roof, suggesting a focus on sustainable energy and modern, eco-conscious manufacturing practices.
global_entity_library.Man in Dark Suit: A man in a dark suit, standing beside Gregor Gries, observing and engaging in discussion about the 3D digital model of the engine projected on the large screen.
global_entity_library.Man in Blue Jacket: A man in a blue jacket, standing beside the Chiron after the run, shaking hands with the driver and clapping, likely a team member or engineer.
global_entity_library.Man in Dark Blazer: A man in a dark blazer with a white shirt, speaking in a modern factory setting with partially assembled white cars behind him, emphasizing the production environment.
global_entity_library.Man in Blue Shirt: A man in a blue striped shirt, standing in the workshop, speaking directly to the camera, providing context on the Bugatti's performance and engine's versatility, stating, 'We build a car that does over 400 km an hour and drives elegantly to the opera in the evening. To do this, you need not only a car that can achieve this, but also an engine that's capable of driving in the summer and winter, in cities and on race tracks.'
global_entity_library.Grand Mansion: A large, elegant, historic-style mansion with white facades, multiple windows, and a grand staircase, serving as a luxurious backdrop for the Bugatti Chiron.
global_entity_library.Man in White Shirt: Formula 1 winner and Colombian racing driver, Juan Pablo Montoya, seen driving the Bugatti Chiron, smiling after stopping, and exiting the vehicle to celebrate the successful world record run, receiving applause and a handshake.
global_entity_library.Technicians in Bugatti Uniforms: Workers in dark blue Bugatti-branded uniforms, one with 'PRODUCTION' and the 'B' logo on the back, actively securing components during assembly, including body panels and carbon fiber parts, identified as '20 elite mechanics' working at '12 high-end production stations in Molsheim' to produce the world's fastest, most exquisite, and most extreme hypercars.
global_entity_library.Rotating Assembly Platform: A circular, motorized platform inside the Bugatti Factory holding the Chiron chassis, allowing technicians to access all sides for precise assembly work.
global_entity_library.Bugatti Factory: A spacious, modern workshop with white floors, large windows, and organized equipment, where the Chiron is assembled on a rotating platform, with carbon fiber parts and machinery visible in the background, described as the 'French sports car manufactory' and an 'absolute superlative'.
global_entity_library.Stephan Winkelmann: A man in a dark suit with white hair, identified as President of Bugatti, standing in front of a grand mansion with a black Bugatti Chiron behind him, gesturing as he speaks in a clear male voice, introducing the Chiron as the 'ultimate hyper sports car' named after a legendary racing driver, and detailing its 8-liter, 16-cylinder engine with up to 1,600 horsepower, while also noting the industry's initial skepticism about building the world's fastest production sports car.
global_entity_library.Digital Animation of Bugatti Chiron: A stylized, rotating 3D model of the Bugatti Chiron in a dark studio environment, with glowing blue and red airflow lines illustrating aerodynamics, and on-screen text highlighting 'REAR WING 1.5 METERS WIDTH' as the wing deploys, while a narrator explains its function in pressing the car onto the asphalt, cooling brakes, and acting as an adaptive airbrake by tilting to 49 degrees after 0.8 seconds of braking.
global_entity_library.Forklift Operator: A technician operating a yellow forklift, moving a partially assembled Chiron chassis within the workshop, wearing a dark blue Bugatti uniform with the logo on the back.
global_entity_library.Oven-like Curing Chamber: A large, industrial chamber with red and yellow lighting, likely used for curing or bonding carbon fiber components of the Chiron chassis. It has a metal frame and mesh walls, with a chassis mounted on a rack inside.
global_entity_library.Christophe Piochon: A man in a dark blazer and white shirt, identified as Member of the Board, speaking directly to the camera in the Bugatti Factory, explaining that the 'unreal' performance of the Bugatti requires 'a lot of work at the development and also at the production level' to be achieved.
global_entity_library.Yellow Forklift: A yellow forklift is not visible in this segment; instead, a lifting mechanism is used to maneuver engine components, indicating a shift in tool usage within the assembly process.
global_entity_library.Engine on Wheeled Platform: A silver, motorized platform with green and black clamps, designed to transport and secure the engine during assembly, being pushed by technicians.
global_entity_library.W16 8.0L Bugatti Chiron Engine: A large, complex engine with visible turbochargers and pipes, partially wrapped in protective plastic, being maneuvered on a wheeled platform during assembly.
global_entity_library.Volkswagen Salzgitter Plant: A large industrial complex with a white roof featuring the VW logo, surrounded by trees with autumn foliage, and an entrance sign reading 'Herzlich willkommen bei Volkswagen Salzgitter,' where the narrator states that 'Since 2005, the engines for the French luxury brand have been hand-built.'
global_entity_library.Gregor Gries: A man in a blue striped shirt, identified as Head of Engine Development, standing in front of a large projection screen, gesturing and discussing a 3D digital model of the engine with another man in a dark suit.
global_entity_library.Lifting Mechanism: A yellow mechanical arm with a rotating platform used to lift and position the heavy engine, featuring adjustable clamps and a control panel.
global_entity_library.Control Panel Interface: A computer monitor with a touchscreen interface, used by a technician to manage the assembly system, featuring a yellow emergency stop button and indicator lights.
global_entity_library.CNC Machining Station: A precision machine tool, referred to as an 'automatic station' in the audio, with multiple tool heads and a control panel, used for processes like oxygen plasma cleaning and black sealant application on the engine pan, before drilling and tapping operations.
global_entity_library.Dominic Stammnitz: A mechanic in a dark blue Bugatti uniform, standing in front of the engine, speaking directly to the camera, with a nameplate graphic identifying him as 'DOMINIC STAMMNITZ, MECHANIC ENGINE CONSTRUCTION'.
global_entity_library.Historical Bugatti Footage: Black-and-white archival clips showing early Bugatti racing cars, drivers, and crowds, with the 'BUGATTI' logo and 'WELT' watermark prominently displayed, highlighting the brand’s 'over 110 years of vehicle production' and enduring philosophy.
global_entity_library.WELT: A watermark in the bottom left corner of the screen, indicating the source of the footage, visible throughout the entire segment.
global_entity_library.Michael Gericke: A man with gray hair and glasses, initially in a dark blue blazer, later in a dark blue Bugatti polo shirt with the brand logo and French tricolor trim, identified by a narrator as 'an acoustic engineer' and 'the sound expert' conducting sound analysis on the W16 8.0L Bugatti Chiron engine. He is seen speaking directly to the camera about engine calibration, stating that 'What the driver's hearing is an entirely unconscious process.'
global_entity_library.Harman PWAC Power Unit: A yellow and blue power supply unit connected to the laptop, labeled 'Harman PWAC', providing power for the testing equipment.
global_entity_library.Panasonic Toughbook: A rugged laptop displaying real-time audio spectrograms and FFT graphs, labeled 'BUGAWOMEWYTL', used for monitoring engine sound data during testing.
global_entity_library.Dr. Conrad Schucht: A man with gray hair and glasses, wearing a dark blue Bugatti polo shirt with the brand logo and French tricolor trim, speaking in a workshop about the necessity of specific acoustic data for vehicle harmony, and later analyzing engine sound data on a Panasonic Toughbook laptop, explaining how rolling noise, window construction, and chassis insulation contribute to the overall perceived noise and a 'consistent concert' for a positive interior experience.
global_entity_library.Driver in Racing Suit: A driver wearing blue Sparco racing gloves and a dark jacket, seen from the interior perspective, gripping the steering wheel and operating the gear lever during the high-speed run. The narrator refers to this individual as 'the expert'.
global_entity_library.Test Track Environment: A wide, paved airfield under an overcast sky, surrounded by green fields and distant mountains, used for the high-speed acceleration run of the Bugatti Chiron. The narrator mentions it as a 'runway' where a 'green light from the tower' is received, accompanied by garbled radio communication.

Its rear spoiler is described as pressing the car onto the asphalt, cooling brakes, and acting as an adaptive airbrake, with its angular position, diffuser flaps, and underbody regulated for safety and stability.

He speaks in French, translated by a narrator, explaining the meticulous process of protecting the Chiron with film during its test drive to ensure a perfect delivery. He details the 350 km test drive, which simulates all driving situations a customer might encounter, including testing at an airfield for speeds not allowed on public roads, ensuring the customer can safely drive the vehicle at its top speed of 400 km/h. He is later seen climbing into the driver's seat for a 5-hour quality test.
